metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Reasoning

**agent_supports = yes.** Mistral Vibe has a family of synchronous, local-only REPL slash commands that never round-trip to the model. Its `CommandRegistry` (`vibe/cli/commands.py:37-176`) registers `/help`, `/status`, `/model`, `/config`, `/thinking`, `/theme`, `/log`, etc. `/help` (`_show_help`, `app.py:2152`) renders `get_help_text()` into a UI widget; `/status` (`_show_status`, `app.py:2378`) formats already-collected `agent_loop.stats` into a widget. Neither calls the LLM. (Note: Vibe has NO `/cost` command — its statistics live under `/status`; the recipe uses `/help` + `/status`, the Vibe analogues of claudecode's `/help` + `/cost`.)

**daemon_capability = full.** This is the load-bearing finding, and Vibe's mechanism differs from claudecode's. In claudecode a `<local-command>` wrapper IS written to the transcript and the parser must filter it (`isLocalCommandContent`). In Vibe the slash command is never written to `messages.jsonl` at all: `_handle_command` (`app.py:1391-1412`) resolves the command via `parse_command`, mounts a UI-only `SlashCommandMessage` echo, invokes the handler, and returns `True` — short-circuiting BEFORE the input is added to the agent-loop message list. Only that message list is persisted, via `SessionLogger.persist_messages` / `save_interaction` (`core/session/session_logger.py:240-262`), which appends only `messages.jsonl` lines for real LLM turns (`save_interaction` early-returns unless a non-system message exists). The rewind code confirms the echo is not a real message (`app.py:3044-3053`: "real user messages, not slash-command echo messages").

Because the daemon's `FilesUnderRoot` Source tails `messages.jsonl`, a synchronous slash command produces NO new transcript line -> NO ParsedEvent -> NO state transition. The session stays in `ready` before, during, and after the slash window. The Vibe parser (`parser.go`) has (and needs) no local-command filter — there is simply nothing to filter. This is exactly the scenario's asserted behavior: state stays `ready`; no `-> working` fires for the slash command itself. Even `/model` (which mutates `config.active_model` in the `meta.json` sidecar) emits no event: the sidecar is read only to ENRICH an existing ParsedEvent (`parser.go:applySidecar`), never to synthesize one, so with no transcript write there is no re-read and no emission.

**driver_capability = gap:wait_turn.** Vibe only materializes `messages.jsonl` after the first real LLM turn (`save_interaction` early-returns on a system-only message list), so the recipe must issue a warm-up turn FIRST to birth an observable session, then fire the slash commands into the settled `ready` window — mirroring claudecode's recipe. That warm-up needs a `wait_turn` step to know the turn completed before measuring the ready invariant. The mistral-vibe interactive driver stubs `wait_turn` (`not_implemented`) and its `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"` omits it, so recipe-lint reports `not-elicited:wait_turn` -> `driver=gap:wait_turn`. `record` ports the real turn-done poll from the reference driver before driving. The `slash` step itself is NOT a gap: `slash` is in `DRIVE_ELICITS` and the driver dispatches `send|slash` to `send_text` (tmux send-keys + Enter). This is the same gap the sibling `session-start` and `model-context-display` cells carry.
caveats: Vibe has no /cost command; the recipe uses /help + /status (both source-confirmed synchronous, no LLM call). Avoid /compact (summarizes via a model call), /clear (clears history — different scenario), /rewind (checkpoint-rewind scenario), /resume (session picker), /teleport (network).

daemon=full is derived from the source path: the slash command short-circuits before persistence, so nothing reaches the tailed messages.jsonl. This is confirmed from vibe source (app.py:_handle_command + session_logger.persist_messages) but the emission-absence is settled only by a live recording — confidence held at 0.85 until record confirms no transcript write during the slash window.
Cost is not asserted: the vibe adapter emits no per-turn cost. Observations pin model=mistral-medium-3.5 (confirmed from live meta.json sidecars) + tokens_nonzero + agent.

sources.note: Vibe parser maps transcript lines by role; a user line opens a turn (working). No line is written for a slash command, so no event is produced — no local-command filter is needed (unlike claudecode).
Source = FilesUnderRoot tailing ~/.vibe/logs/session/<id>/messages.jsonl; the daemon only observes what is appended to that transcript.
_handle_command: parse_command -> mount SlashCommandMessage (UI echo only) -> run handler -> return True, short-circuiting before the input joins the agent-loop message list.

persist_messages / save_interaction append messages.jsonl only for the agent-loop message list (early-returns on a system-only list); a slash command never enters that list, so it never reaches the transcript.
_show_help (get_help_text -> widget) and _show_status (app.py:2378, formats agent_loop.stats -> widget): both purely local, no LLM call.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; wait_turn is not_implemented -> recipe-lint reports not-elicited:wait_turn. slash is elicited (send|slash -> send_text), so the slash step is not a gap.

preconditions: vibe CLI (v2.19.0) on PATH and authenticated (Mistral API key)
irrlichd is recording (isolated mode or --attach against a --record daemon)
The chosen slash commands (/help, /status) must remain local-only in the running vibe version — if a future release routes either through the model the scenario premise breaks
setup: Driver launches a fresh interactive vibe session under a fresh cwd; a warm-up turn materializes messages.jsonl so the session births in the recording
Avoid /clear (clears history — session-reset scenario), /compact (LLM call), /rewind (checkpoint scenario), /resume (picker), /teleport (network)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: warm

verify: events.jsonl shows the vibe session entering ready at birth and a single ready->working->ready cycle driven by the warm-up turn only
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to working or waiting during the /help + /status window
messages.jsonl gains exactly one user line and one assistant turn (the warm-up); neither /help nor /status appears as a transcript line
